results.title: MuleRun: Raise an AI that actually learns how you work - Product Hunt
results.content:
Report

Ines Defirenza

MuleRun

MuleRun

Maker

@jpeggdev This is a genuinely exciting use case and the architecture maps onto it better than you might expect. MuleRun's 24/7 dedicated VM means you can run sustained, multi-pass optimization loops without babysitting them — set up the evaluation criteria, define what "better" looks like, and let it iterate across 100 passes while you're away. The persistent environment means intermediate results, candidate states, and benchmarks all survive across sessions, so nothing gets lost between runs. For 3D rendering specifically — culling strategies, shading path variants, draw call reduction — the agent can execute, benchmark, log results, and feed winning candidates back into the next generation without manual intervention at each step. [...] For your specific developer workflow, I'd recommend trying our "Coding & Building" mode — it's designed for hosting and running services 24/7 on your dedicated VM. Would love to hear how it fits into your stack. Feel free to jump in and give it a spin.

Report

Ines Defirenza

MuleRun

MuleRun

Maker

@slavaakulov This is exactly the kind of use case we get most excited about — and your framing is spot on. Context-awareness without manual setup is the hard problem, and it's precisely what MuleRun's architecture is designed to address. [...] Personal Investment Assistant: Traders build agents that monitor markets 24/7, execute strategies, and proactively initiate post-trade reviews — learning your risk preferences over time. Check it out

Always-on Content Creation: Creators use MuleRun to continuously generate comic/drama scripts — the agent keeps working even when the laptop is closed. See examples

The magic is that users aren't just running automations — they're raising a self-evolving digital partner that proactively works for them 24/7. What's your use case? We'd love to help you get started!

Report

Simon Kotlerman

results.title: RunLLM: AI that doesn't just respond—it resolves - Product Hunt
results.content: It's been incredible going from the foundations we laid out in RunLLM v1 to a complete agent-centric operating mode that has more flexibility in the actions it can take in order to solve customer requests. The tricky part at the core of all LLM-powered applications is making sure that your agent stays within guardrails even as you increase the scope of the actions that it can take. We've iterated a lot on this and I think we've built out something really special that gives users insight into each step the agent is taking. I'm also especially excited about the tool use integrations where agents can now analyze logs and telemetry data. The combination of the two makes RunLLM v2 feel like a big step in the direction towards making a RunLLM agent a core part of your team!
